problem.title: Implement Queue using Stacks
problem.description: Implement a first-in-first-out (FIFO) queue using only two stacks.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Two Stacks
solutions.description: Use two stacks: an input stack for push operations and an output stack for pop/peek operations. When the output stack is empty and a pop or peek is needed, all elements from the input stack are transferred to the output stack, reversing their order and exposing the oldest element at the top. The key insight is that a lazy transfer strategy gives O(1) amortized cost per operation because each element is moved at most once.
